g.text: The state is one of 2 models:
- State A = State B always (Strong Consistency model)
Whenever client access any node, he get the same data
- State A and state B will be eventually equal (eventual consistency)
Client can get inconsistent data depending on the node called
This mode is called Partitioning or Sharding
The common knowledge is split across multiple nodes
The first case achieves availability through providing more nodes
having the same state so if one node goes down the other can server
The second case achieves availability through data partitioning, imaging the state
become so large that can't be stored within a single node we need to split data across
for the shards , what if the node containing State A goes down then this node's can't be reached
any more, we need replication to achieve availability of the single partition/shard. So if Not
now we need to ensure consistency across the partitions and replicas
